# Acidification in Biological Systems

This document describes acidification processes in microbial fermentation systems.

## Biological acidification

Acidification through microbial fermentation is a natural process where bacteria and fungi lower the pH of a medium by producing organic acids. Lactobacillus species are common acidifiers in fermented foods, metabolizing sugars into lactic acid. This reduces pH and preserves the food while developing characteristic flavors. The fermentation process is anaerobic and continues as long as food substrate is available.

## Storage

Acidified foods should be stored in cool, dry conditions away from direct sunlight. Glass containers are preferred to maintain product quality over time.
